title: Impetigenic Scabies Mimicking Kerion Tinea Capitis in an 8-Year-Old Boy
description: An 8-year-old boy with a twin presented with 2 weeks of painful erythematous scalp nodules, pus discharge, and alopecia in the parieto-occipital region after contact with stray cats. KOH preparation and fungal culture were negative; microscopy confirmed Sarcoptes scabiei mite, establishing a diagnosis of impetigenic scabies. Both twins were treated with topical permethrin, cetirizine, and amoxicillin with complete hair regrowth by follow-up. Derived from AGBonnet augmented-clinical-notes dataset (idx 104203).
notes: Derived from dataset row idx 104203. The original dataset row is copied into case_08_source.json. All patient data comes from the synthetic augmented-clinical-notes dataset. The key diagnostic challenge is distinguishing impetigenic scabies from kerion-type tinea capitis; the mite identification on skin scraping microscopy is the confirming finding.
